Live Community Volunteer Opportunities
Posted directly by member nonprofits — North Mecklenburg & South Iredell. Sign up to help right now.

Student Volunteer Registration — Under 18

Free to register. Get matched with a nonprofit, earn service hours, points, and badges. AI generates a personalized intro letter to your matched organization. All data saves to Google Sheet.

⚠️ Parent/guardian consent may be required for under-18 placements.

Adult Volunteer Registration — 18+

Free to register. Find flexible opportunities across North Mecklenburg & South Iredell. AI generates a personalized matching letter to your best-fit organizations.

🏆
LIVE — Community Challenge
Updates in real time as your community volunteers

Who Will Lead
Lake Norman?

Hours. Donations. In-Kind items. Auction contributions. Every act of service earns your community points — and a shot at the top of the board. Students challenge schools. Employers challenge each other. Faith groups, clubs, and neighborhoods go head to head. The whole community is watching.

🎓
Student Challenge
Compete for Most Hours, Top Service, and NHS recognition. Your rank appears on every school's leaderboard.
🏫
School Showdown
Which school gives the most back? NHS chapters, Key Clubs, and Beta Clubs fight for the #1 spot.
💼
Employer Challenge
Rally your team, log volunteer hours together, and dominate the employer board. Bragging rights included.
⛪
Faith vs. Faith
Which congregation serves the most? Every hour your members volunteer counts toward your church's total.
🏘️
Neighborhood Pride
Cornelius vs. Huntersville vs. Davidson vs. Mooresville. Which community gives back the most?

🌎 NOT YOUR AVERAGE MEETING

LINK Lake Norman Quarterly
Nonprofit Connection

Collaboration · Impact · Shared Resources

📅 Thursday, April 23, 2026 ⏰ 9:00 AM – 10:30 AM 📍 The Serve Pickleball + Kitchen, Cornelius NC 🗺️ 20220 Chartown Dr, Cornelius 28031

Join us in person for the LINK Lake Norman Quarterly Nonprofit Connection! Meet other local nonprofit leaders face-to-face, share ideas, build meaningful partnerships, and make a positive impact in our community. Come ready to connect, collaborate, and multiply your mission.

🤝 True collaboration — not just networking
💡 Resource sharing, roundtables & cross-org partnerships
🏫 Open to nonprofits, schools, civic orgs & community leaders
⏱️ 1.5 hours · In person · Cornelius NC
🎟️ Register on Eventbrite 📋 View Full Event Details ✉️ Membership Info

Quarterly event · The Serve Pickleball + Kitchen · Cornelius NC · Join the LINK Lake Norman network for future event announcements.

About the Quarterly Connection™

The LINK Lake Norman Nonprofit & Community Quarterly Connection is the region's premier gathering for organizations making a difference in North Mecklenburg and South Iredell. Unlike typical networking events, this is a working session — built around real collaboration, shared challenges, and collective impact.

🤝 Who Should Attend
Nonprofit leaders, executive directors, board members, civic organization heads, school administrators, government liaisons, and community advocates.
💡 What Happens
Facilitated roundtables, resource-sharing segments, cross-org partnership introductions, community data updates, and open collaboration time.
🎯 The Goal
Leave with at least one new partnership, one shared resource connection, and a clearer picture of the community landscape across North Mecklenburg.
📬 Stay Connected
Email [email protected] to RSVP, request membership info, or get on the quarterly invite list.

🔐

Member-Only Nonprofit Concierge Resources

Your $200 annual membership unlocks the full LINK Lake Norman resource library — everything you need to start, grow, fund, and sustain your nonprofit in North Carolina. Curated, current, and always updated.

🔒 Active members: log in via My Portal to access all resources

How to Start a Nonprofit in NC

Step-by-step guide: articles of incorporation, 501(c)(3) application, bylaws, board formation, EIN, state registration, and charitable solicitation license — in the right order.

IRS 501(c)(3) Application (Form 1023) NC Secretary of State — Business Registration NC Attorney General — Charitable Registration IRS Form SS-4 (EIN Application)

Compliance & Annual Requirements

Stay legal and in good standing: IRS Form 990, NC annual report, charitable solicitation renewal, board meeting minutes, conflict of interest policies, and audit requirements.

IRS Form 990 Guide NC Annual Report Filing Nonprofit Financial Policies Template BoardSource — Board Meeting Templates

Fundraising & Grant Writing

How to get donations, write winning grants, cultivate major donors, plan fundraising events, and stay compliant with NC charitable solicitation laws.

Candid / Foundation Directory — Grant Search NC Fundraising Compliance Guidelines Grants.gov — Federal Grant Opportunities Foundation for the Carolinas — Local Grants

Event Sponsorships & In-Kind

How to attract event sponsors, create sponsor packages, approach local businesses, manage in-kind donations, and run auctions and raffles legally in NC.

NC Raffle & Lottery Guidelines for Nonprofits IRS In-Kind Donation Acknowledgment Rules NC Council of Nonprofits — Best Practices

Board Governance & Templates

Meeting minute templates, board policies, conflict of interest forms, officer role descriptions, term limits, quorum rules, and Robert's Rules basics — all NC-specific.

NC Center for Nonprofits — Member Resources Board Roles & Responsibility Guide IRS Compliance Guide for 501(c)(3) Public Charities

Top Nonprofit Resources

The best free tools for nonprofits: project management, donor CRM, volunteer tracking, website builders, email marketing, and accounting — many free for nonprofits.

TechSoup — Free software for nonprofits Canva for Nonprofits — Free design tools VolunteerHub — Volunteer management Candid/GuideStar — Nonprofit profiles & data

How to Dissolve a Nonprofit

If it's time to close: NC dissolution process, IRS final return, notifying the Attorney General, distributing assets, and handling outstanding obligations properly.

NC Articles of Dissolution IRS — Terminating Tax-Exempt Status

Local & County Resources

Mecklenburg County and Iredell County resources, community foundations, government contacts, and local nonprofit support organizations.

Mecklenburg County Community Support Services Iredell County Government Resources Foundation for the Carolinas — NP Support American Job Centers — Workforce Resources
🚀

🎁 In-Kind Donations, Sponsors & Auction Items
Available items, sponsor opportunities, and fundraiser contributions for LKN member nonprofits

Available In-Kind Donations

Items and services donated by businesses and community members — available to any LINK Lake Norman member nonprofit. Contact LINK to claim.

Available Now

Office Furniture — 6 Desks, 12 Chairs

Gently used office furniture donated by a Huntersville business. Great for new or growing nonprofits.

Available Now

Commercial Kitchen Time — 8 hrs/month

Local restaurant offering kitchen access for food nonprofits needing prep space in Cornelius.

Available Now

Marketing Photography — 4-hour session

Professional photographer donating a session for nonprofit event or program documentation.

Available Now

Storage Space — 10x10 climate-controlled unit

Donated by Morningstar Storage for a member nonprofit needing supply or equipment storage.

Auction & Raffle Items Available

Items available for nonprofit fundraising events — auctions, raffles, and benefit dinners. All items donated by LKN businesses and community partners.

Auction Item

Lake Norman Dinner for 4 — Value $250

Gift certificate to a local lakefront restaurant. Perfect for gala or benefit dinner auction.

Raffle Item

Weekend Pontoon Boat Rental

Full-day Lake Norman boat rental donated by a local marina. Fantastic raffle prize.

Auction Item

Home Services Package — Value $500

Landscaping + cleaning services donated by LKN home service businesses. Event-ready.

Donate an Item or Service

Are you a business with something to contribute? Donate auction, raffle, or in-kind items to support our LKN nonprofits.

Post Your Nonprofit's In-Kind Need

Member nonprofits: post what you need — supplies, services, equipment, volunteers with specific skills. LINK matches you with donors and businesses.

Business Partnership Inquiry

Tell us about your goals and we'll build the right partnership for your business.

Your logo will appear on the Community Hub, leaderboard, and partner materials.
Company overview, services brochure, or one-pager for our partner directory.

🎁 Donate an Item, Service or Sponsorship

Businesses can donate in-kind goods, services, auction items, or raffle prizes directly through LINK Lake Norman. Set your inventory, limits, and item type — we'll automatically connect you with the right nonprofit and send a professional introduction email to both parties.

+ Add a Donation Item or Sponsorship
Shows on the in-kind board next to your donation listing.
When you submit, LINK Lake Norman will review your items, list them on the In-Kind & Sponsors board, and automatically send a professional introduction email connecting you with interested nonprofits. You control inventory and limits.
🤝

Help Us Keep Building a Connected Community

LINK Lake Norman is the connective tissue between nonprofits, businesses, volunteers, and community across North Mecklenburg and South Iredell. We do the leg work — building portals, matching volunteers, curating resources, and connecting people to purpose — so nonprofits can focus on their mission.

🌐

Become a Nonprofit Member

$200/year gives your org a full concierge platform. The best investment in your community impact.

💼

Become a Business Sponsor

Sponsorships from $500/year. Get visibility, impact, and a seat at the table in North Mecklenburg.

❤️

Donate to the Community Fund

Contribute to the shared pool — divided equally among all member nonprofits at year-end.

📣

Spread the Word

Share LINK Lake Norman with nonprofits, volunteers, and businesses in your network. Every connection matters.

🌐
PARTNERSHIP OPPORTUNITY

Build a Community Hub
for Your Region

Certified Community Partners receive this badge to display on their website, social media, and printed materials.

LINK Lake Norman is more than a local platform - it's a replicable model for connecting students, nonprofits, businesses, and schools in any community. We partner with schools, districts, and civic leaders outside Lake Norman to build fully branded, locally operated community hubs powered by the same platform.

Your Region. Your Brand.
We build a hub customized for your community - your local nonprofits, your schools, your opportunities. LINK LKN provides the infrastructure and strategy.
Schools & Students First
Students earn hours, build verified service portfolios, and connect to local nonprofits where they live - not just where the buses go. Every hub includes a school district license.
Google Sheets Backend
Every hub has its own Google Sheet - all volunteer signups, hours logged, nonprofit applications, and event RSVPs flow in automatically. Your data stays yours.
Revenue Ready
Each hub generates its own revenue from nonprofit memberships, school licenses, and business sponsors. Setup fee + annual platform fee keeps it sustainable for everyone.
Hub Partner Pricing
$2,500 setup fee + $1,200/year platform license - includes school district license for your region
$2,500 + $1,200/yr
Start the Conversation School District Inquiry

Ask About Local Community Needs

AI-powered insights using real North Mecklenburg and Mecklenburg County data.

📋 How Nonprofits Update Their Portal

Step 1 — Apply & Create Password
Go to the Join as Nonprofit tab. Complete the application and create your unique portal password. Keep it safe — you'll use it to log in anytime.
Step 2 — Log In Here
Enter your org email and the password you created. Your portal opens instantly — showing your profile, stats, matched volunteers, and controls.
Step 3 — Update Your Info
Inside your portal: update mission text, upload a new logo, change your website link, edit volunteer/donate URLs, and post new opportunities — all in real time.
Step 4 — Post Opportunities
Use the Community Board tab to post live volunteer opportunities with slots, frequency, and description. These appear on the board immediately and link back to your org.
Need Help? Email Us

Portal Login

Nonprofits, groups, schools, and admins all log in here. Use your registered email and the password you created. Admins use the master admin password.

🌐 Nonprofit?
Apply via the Join as Nonprofit tab — your portal activates after payment.
🏫 Group / School?
Register via the Groups & Schools tab — use your group email & password.
⚙️ Admin?
Use the master admin password to access all portals, data, and controls.
🔒
Admin Access
LINK Lake Norman staff only

Admin — Nonprofit Directory Management

Edit any card, upload logos, manage all 30 member organizations.

LogoOrganizationCategoryWebsiteActions

Google Sheets Webhook

Apps Script setup: Extensions → Apps Script → paste webhook code → Deploy → Web app → Access: Anyone → copy URL above.

GitHub Deployment

1. Create repo at github.com → name: link-lkn-hub
2. Upload link-lkn-hub.html → rename to index.html
3. Settings → Pages → Source: main branch / root
4. Live at: https://yourusername.github.io/link-lkn-hub
5. To add more logos: upload to /logos/ folder → update logo: field in NPS array
📊 Form Submissions
Live data from your Google Sheet — private to admin only
Click "Load Data" to view submissions

FOR SCHOOLS · TEAMS · CLUBS · ORGANIZATIONS

on Spreadsheets.

LINK Lake Norman gives every NHS chapter, sports team, club, and civic group a dedicated portal — with collective hour tracking, a group leaderboard, automated service transcripts, and group event management. One place. No spreadsheets.

Get a School License →

Register Your Group

Create a group portal for your school, team, or organization. Each member still gets their own individual portal and leaderboard ranking — but you also get a group dashboard showing collective hours, events, and impact.

Members join your group by registering individually (Student or Adult portal) and entering your group password. All their hours roll up to your group dashboard automatically.

📊 Group Dashboard

See total hours, top members, active volunteers, and opportunities completed — all as a group. Perfect for NHS advisors, team coaches, and club leaders.

🏆 Group Leaderboard

Groups compete on a separate leaderboard by total hours. Schools, teams, and clubs can see how they rank against each other — driving friendly competition for community impact.

📋 Group Events

Post group-specific volunteer events visible only to your members, or open them to the wider community. Manage sign-ups and track attendance all in one place.

📄 Service Transcripts

Generate downloadable service hour transcripts for individual members — verified, dated, and signed by LINK LKN. Perfect for NHS, college applications, and scholarship submissions.

🏫

School & District Licenses Available

Replace spreadsheets and manual NHS tracking with a full service hour management platform. Annual licenses for schools and districts include unlimited student portals, advisor dashboards, automated transcripts, and reporting — everything a school needs to manage community service.

Keep the core platform free for students to drive adoption. Monetize the organizational and business side — nonprofit memberships, school licenses, business sponsorships, premium profiles, and grant funding. The sweet spot: 30 nonprofits at $200 = $6,000 · 10 school licenses at $499 = $4,990 · 3 business sponsors at $1,500+ = $4,500+ · That's $15,000+ annually before events, grants, or premium student profiles.
